title: Soft-Body Stork Landing on Nemetona's Jelly Shrine
description: A stork with soft-body deformable wings descends toward a jelly-like shrine of Nemetona that wobbles and deforms on impact. The user controls wind direction, wing stiffness, landing angle, shrine elasticity, and bounce dampening to achieve a stable perch without the stork sliding off the wobbling surface.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with GLTFLoader and OrbitControls from CDN. Load stork.glb and nemetona.glb. Scene setup: place the Nemetona statue on a ground plane at origin, scale it to roughly 2 units tall. Position the stork 8 units above and 4 units to the right, angled downward. Use a perspective camera at (0,5,12) looking at origin. Add hemisphere light (sky 0x88ccff, ground 0x443322, intensity 1.2) and a directional light with soft shadows. Implement a custom soft-body simulation: for the stork, compute vertex displacement on wing mesh vertices using a spring-damper model—store rest positions, apply gravity plus wind force, and resolve with Verlet integration each frame. Clamp max displacement to prevent explosion. For the Nemetona shrine, treat its surface vertices as a jelly mass: on collision with the stork's bounding box, push nearby vertices downward proportional to impact velocity, then spring them back with configurable stiffness and damping. Create a right-side GUI panel with these validated controls: (1) Wind Direction slider 0-360 degrees, (2) Wind Strength slider 0.0-2.0, (3) Wing Stiffness slider 0.01-1.0 controlling spring constant of stork wing vertices, (4) Shrine Elasticity slider 0.01-1.0 controlling how much the Nemetona mesh deforms on impact, (5) Bounce Damping slider 0.0-1.0 controlling energy loss per bounce, (6) Drop Height slider 3-15 units, (7) a Launch button that releases the stork. Validate that wing stiffness times shrine elasticity is below 0.8 to prevent instability—show a red warning if violated. On launch, animate the stork falling under gravity plus wind. When the stork bounding box intersects the shrine, trigger jelly deformation and bounce. Display a status bar showing impact velocity, number of bounces, and whether the stork settled (velocity below 0.05 for 60 frames). Add a Reset button to restore initial positions and vertex data. All vertex buffers must use needsUpdate=true after modification.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  storkPositionY: number,         // current Y coordinate of stork center of mass in world space
  storkVelocityY: number,         // vertical velocity component of stork in units per second
  storkRotationX: number,         // pitch rotation of stork body in radians
  windDirection: number,         // wind direction in degrees (0-360)
  windStrength: number,         // magnitude of wind force applied to wing vertices (0.0-2.0)
  wingStiffness: number,         // spring constant for wing deformation physics (0.01-1.0)
  shrineElasticity: number,         // spring constant for shrine jelly surface recovery (0.01-1.0)
  bounceDampening: number,         // damping coefficient for shrine vertex oscillations (0.0-1.0)
  maxShrineDeformation: number,         // maximum vertex displacement of shrine surface in units
  isStorkLanded: boolean,         // true when stork has made contact with shrine surface
  shrineStabilityScore: number,         // measure of shrine surface calmness (0.0-1.0, higher is more stable)
  storkSlipDistance: number,         // horizontal distance stork has slid on shrine surface since landing
}
